description.value:
Den Den Mushi Item 3

Common

A small snail kept in a dedicated cradle on a ship or in a building, serving as the primary long-range communication device. It rings audibly when a call comes in and mimics the caller's voice and expression. Any creature can pick up the call by touching the snail and speaking.

Long-Range Communication: As a single action, a creature touching the Den Den Mushi can initiate or receive a voice call with any other Den Den Mushi (including Baby variants) at unlimited range, provided neither snail is in a jamming field. The call is two-way and fully audible to anyone within 10 feet of the snail.

Voice Mimicry: The snail perfectly replicates the caller's voice. Listeners cannot identify the transmission as a relay through mundane means (DC 20 Perception to notice).

Non-Combatant: If threatened, the Den Den Mushi retreats into its shell (Hardness 4, HP 6, BT 3), cutting any active call. It emerges again once the threat passes.

Standard issue for any ship sailing the Grand Line. Pirate crews, merchant vessels, and naval warships all rely on them.
description.gm: A ship's Den Den Mushi can call any other snail in the world — there is no bond restriction like the Baby variant. However, the caller must know the 'number' (the identity) of the target snail; calling a random snail requires GM adjudication. Den Den Mushi are generally treated as neutral parties — they relay whatever they hear without judgment.
